entries.title: Gate Tension
entries.summary: Arrivals are being sized up before anyone decides whether to welcome them or blame them.
entries.detail: This could work as a social pressure scene first. Watchful locals, guarded questions, and a settlement already stretched too thin can all come forward before anyone treats it like a fixed fight.

entries.title: Trade Problem
entries.summary: A shopkeeper or trader needs help with a shortage, dispute, or dangerous supply route.
entries.detail: This could be introduced through requests, tense bargaining, or the visible absence of something people rely on. It should feel like a problem the party can engage with however they choose.

entries.title: {biome_name} pressure in the route ahead
entries.summary: The local environment, bad visibility, and resident threats all suggest the route is already under strain.
entries.detail: This could work as mounting exploratory pressure. Signs, sounds, shifting space, and the sense that something living or unstable is already shaping the route matter more than a forced combat start.

entries.title: Hazard Complication
entries.detail: This could show up as an immediate complication in the route itself. Use the hazard to force a choice, split attention, or make an already-tense scene harder, without treating it as a separate mandatory encounter.
